prompt: I have an Adams model with a slider (marker 5 on part 2, ground marker is 1). I need to write a user subroutine in C that grabs the Y displacement once per iteration and makes it available to my VFOSUB so I don't recompute it every time the force is evaluated. Can you write the CBKSUB and show me how the VFOSUB would read the cached value?
assertions: CBKSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsCbksub* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params
CBKSUB includes adams_c_Callback forward declaration typedef
CBKSUB uses ev_ITERATION_BEG enum constant, not a raw integer like case 4
CBKSUB handles ev_PRIVATE_EVENT1/2 (ignores them without reading data[])
CBKSUB includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
VFOSUB uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter) or correct pointer-based signature
VFOSUB includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
VFOSUB is dflag-aware: uses cache when dflag==0, falls back to direct SYSARY when dflag==1
Both files use c_errmes for error handling
Build command uses mdi.bat (Windows) or mdi -c (Linux), not raw cl/gcc/ifort
Agent offers to compile the code (or runs the build) rather than only providing manual build instructions
Manual Start Menu / Adams Command Prompt instructions are presented as a fallback for when agent compilation fails, not as the primary workflow
